data.title: Trump dismisses EU trade deal worries over Greenland threat - report
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump recently expressed confidence that his tariff threats over Greenland are unlikely to derail a trade deal with Europe, Bloomberg reported

“I doubt it,” Trump said at a White House press conference on Tuesday when asked if tariffs would prompt the EU to renege on investment pledges. “They need that agreement very badly with us. They really do, they fought very hard to get it. So I doubt that.” When asked how far he was willing to go to secure the island, he said “you’ll find out.”

Trump warned that the current 10% tariffs starting February 1, 2026, on goods from eight European countries—Denmark, Sweden, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Finland, the UK, and Norway—rising to 25% by June unless the U.S. secures control of Greenland.

European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen called the threats a "mistake" violating the prior deal. “The European Union and the United States have agreed to a trade deal last July,” von der Leyen said. “In politics as in business, a deal is a deal. And when friends shake hands, it must mean something.”

data.title: Cadre Holdings raises dividend by 5.3% to $0.10
data.description:
• Cadre Holdings (CDRE) declares $0.10/share quarterly dividend, 5.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.095.
  • Forward yield 0.94%
  • Payable Feb. 13; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 5.3% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.095 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See CDRE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: ARK Invest adds new positions in VELO and SNPS
data.description:
ARK Invest disclosed on Tuesday that it has added a new position in Velo3D (VELO) to its ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), expanding the fund’s exposure as part of its ongoing portfolio rebalancing strategy.

Cathie Wood’s investment manager Ark Invest also has added a new  position in Synopsys (SNPS) to its $PRNT fund, the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF, signaling increased exposure to semiconductor software as part of the fund’s latest portfolio adjustments on January 20, 2026.

ARK Invest added 6,608 shares of Synopsys (SNPS), representing 4.59% of the Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF, valued at approximately $3.41 million.

 

More on Velo3D, Inc., ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF, etc.

data.title: Alibaba joins hands with China’s top nuclear firm to power AI expansion - report
Teck Resources meets 2025 copper output guidance as Q4 copper sales lag; cuts Antamina zinc forecast
data.description:
Canada’s Teck Resources (TECK) said on Tuesday that it delivered 453,500 tonnes of copper production in 2025, supported by strong fourth-quarter performance across its operations, even as adverse weather and sea conditions temporarily weighed on Q4 sales.

Copper sales in Q4 2025 were lower than production, primarily due to a short-term build-up in inventory at Quebrada Blanca ((QB)) resulting from weather and sea conditions in December, which delayed shipments into early 2026, the company added.

Quebrada Blanca delivered a strong fourth-quarter performance, producing 55,400 tonnes of copper.

The miner's 2025 annual zinc in concentrate production of 565,000 tonnes, meanwhile, was at the higher end of the guidance range. Zinc in concentrate sales were 157,200 tonnes in the fourth quarter, with all Red Dog shipments completed early in the quarter, in line with the normal seasonality of sales.

Looking ahead to 2026, Teck Resources (TECK) said there have been no changes to its previously disclosed production guidance for 2026–2028, issued on October 7, across all Teck-operated sites.

The updates come as Anglo American (AAUKF) (NGLOY) and Teck Resources (TECK) are headed for antitrust approval in the European Union after the regulator signaled the absence of competition concerns for their $50 billion merger.

However, the company lowered its 2026 annual zinc in concentrate production guidance for Antamina to 35,000–45,000 tonnes from 55,000–65,000 tonnes, reflecting an updated mine plan finalized in the fourth quarter of 2025.
